description_long: This bollard features a vertically striped cylindrical post with alternating black and white bands, topped by a decorative finial and a circular emblem reminiscent of the London Underground logo. Extending horizontally from the upper section is a rectangular signboard, which appears to display transit or directional information. The base is flared and robust, suggesting stability and resistance to impact. The post itself is constructed from what appears to be metal, likely cast iron or steel, with a painted finish for visibility and weather resistance. The signboard is likely made of metal or a durable composite, with printed or embossed text. The overall design is both functional and iconic, serving as a wayfinding or access control point in urban environments.
description: A striped cylindrical bollard with a decorative finial, circular emblem, and attached signboard, designed for urban wayfinding and access control.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular emblem, decorative finial, and the signboard with visible text and details.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the cylindrical post and finial, with the signboard edge-on and not visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the signboard and the striped post, with the emblem and finial visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the post and finial, signboard edge-on and not visible, emphasizing the striped pattern.
